title: crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis
intro: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis, commonly known as the New Stream Hawthorn, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The New Stream Hawthorn has been traditionally used to support heart health, and its health benefits include lowering blood pressure, improving circulation, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Its therapeutic actions are attributed to its cardiotonic, vasodilatory, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to improve cardiovascular function and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of the New Stream Hawthorn include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardio-protective effects.
benefits: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis has many benefits, such as its potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties to protect against cell damage and infections. This plant has also been shown to have cardiovascular benefits, including lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke. Additionally, it may possess neuroprotective properties, which could help prevent or slow the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's, improving cognitive function and overall quality of life. By incorporating Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced well-being, ultimately leading to a more fulfilling and productive life.
actions: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis has many therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against cell damage, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infections. Its antiviral and antifungal properties can also aid in the treatment of various diseases, including herpes and fungal infections. Additionally, the plant's diuretic and expectorant properties can help alleviate conditions such as urinary tract infections and bronchitis, making it easier for people to breathe and urinate. By reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis can improve people's daily lives by helping them manage chronic conditions and maintain a healthy quality of life.
constituents: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin), phenolic acids (chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid), and triterpenoids (ursolic acid and oleanolic acid), which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective effects, improving heart health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. The flavonoids in Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis also have been found to have anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, which can improve the quality of life for cancer patients. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of this plant's constituents can help protect against oxidative stress, improving overall well-being and potentially reducing the risk of age-related diseases.
parts: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, bark, and fruits, which contain various bioactive compounds. The leaves of Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis contain flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flowers of the plant are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have anti-aging and anti-cancer properties. The fruits of Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis contain polyphenolic compounds, including epicatechin and procyanidins, which have been reported to have cardiovascular protective effects.
side_effects: Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, due to its high tannin content. Ingesting large quantities can also lead to allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Some individuals may experience cardiovascular problems, including increased heart rate and blood pressure, when consuming the plant in excessive amounts. Furthermore, using Crataegus macracantha var. neofluvialis for an extended period may interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions like kidney or liver disease.
